summary: Deduplicate the identical KVCreateClient / OCCreateClient trace-and-delegate bodies by extracting a single shared helper CreateObjectClientWithTrace into src/datasystem/c_api/util.{h,cpp}; both extern "C" wrappers become one-line delegations. Both exported ABI symbols (bound by Go cgo) are preserved; CreateObjectClient stays an unchanged pure factory. No new C++ test harness — existing Go binding tests plus compilation gate correctness.

ledger.what: Both KVCreateClient and OCCreateClient are extern "C" ABI symbols consumed by external language bindings, so both must be preserved; neither may be deleted or renamed.
ledger.why: The dedup must not collapse the two public symbols into one, or it breaks the cross-language ABI.
ledger.what_i_did: Verified extern "C" in kv_client_c_wrapper.h:26 and object_client_c_wrapper.h:26, and confirmed Go cgo binds C.KVCreateClient (go/kv/kv_client.go:147) and C.OCCreateClient (go/object/object_client.go:152); kept both entry points and deduplicated only their bodies.

blast_radius.why: Removing or renaming an exported symbol breaks the cross-language ABI and every downstream binding build (Go/Python/Java).

reversibility.why: A shipped ABI break requires coordinated fixes across all downstream language bindings.

ledger.revisit_if: A maintainer confirms one symbol is genuinely unused and may be dropped.

ledger.what: Extraction strategy: add a dedicated shared helper CreateObjectClientWithTrace in util.{h,cpp}; both wrappers delegate to it.
ledger.why: Gives the trace-and-create logic a single named, documented home while leaving CreateObjectClient's factory contract untouched; chosen over moving TraceGuard into CreateObjectClient (L4) and over symbol aliasing (L6).
ledger.what_i_did: Specified the new 17-param void* helper and rewrote both wrapper bodies as one-line delegations to it in the plan.

blast_radius.why: util.h/util.cpp are shared by the kv, object, and stream wrappers, so a new symbol there is broadly visible across the c_api layer.

reversibility.why: Pure internal refactor with no persisted state or exported-symbol change; trivially revertible.

ledger.revisit_if: Review prefers option B (fold TraceGuard into CreateObjectClient) for minimality.

ledger.what: Do not add a new C++ unit-test harness; rely on existing Go binding tests plus compilation to satisfy 'add/keep tests'.
ledger.why: No C++ unit-test harness exists for the c_api wrappers today, the change is pure behaviour-preserving delegation, and both symbols are already exercised via go/kv/kv_client_test.go and go/object/object_client_test.go.
ledger.what_i_did: Scoped testing to 'existing Go tests still pass + both datasystem_c and datasystem_c_static targets compile' in the plan's Task 4.

blast_radius.why: Test-only scope; no production code affected by this decision.

reversibility.why: A dedicated regression test can be added later without undoing any production change.

ledger.revisit_if: A c_api C++ unit-test target is later added, or a maintainer requires a dedicated regression test.

ledger.what: CreateObjectClient has no callers besides the two wrappers, and KVClient_p/ObjectClient_p are both typedef void* identical to its void* return, so a single void*-returning helper serves both without cast churn.
ledger.why: Justifies one shared helper rather than two type-specialized variants.
ledger.what_i_did: Grepped the repo for CreateObjectClient (only the two wrappers plus its own decl/def) and confirmed the typedefs at kv_client_c_wrapper.h:33 and object_client_c_wrapper.h:32.

blast_radius.why: Confined to the create-client entry points; no other callers to affect.

reversibility.why: Assumption is easy to re-check and the helper is trivially adjustable if a new caller appears.

ledger.revisit_if: A new direct caller of CreateObjectClient is introduced.

ledger.what: Keep CreateObjectClient unchanged as a pure factory with no tracing side effect; the TraceGuard lives only in the new helper.
ledger.why: CreateObjectClient is declared and documented in util.h; folding a trace side effect into it would silently broaden its contract for any future caller.
ledger.what_i_did: Placed the TraceGuard exclusively in CreateObjectClientWithTrace and left CreateObjectClient's body untouched.

blast_radius.why: Additive change; the existing factory contract is preserved so nothing downstream is affected.

reversibility.why: Trace behaviour could be relocated later with a one-line move.

ledger.revisit_if: Tracing is later desired at the factory level for all CreateObjectClient callers.

ledger.what: Reject the __attribute__((alias(...))) symbol-aliasing approach that would collapse both public symbols onto one definition.
ledger.why: It is compiler-specific (GCC/Clang), fragile across the C ABI and cross-language boundary, and harder to read than a plain delegation.
ledger.what_i_did: Excluded aliasing; both symbols remain real functions that delegate to the shared helper.

blast_radius.why: Choosing plain delegation over aliasing has no effect beyond the two wrapper definitions.

reversibility.why: Aliasing could be adopted later if ever justified; nothing here forecloses it.

ledger.revisit_if: Binary size or symbol-count constraints justify aliasing and the toolchain is pinned.

ledger.what: TraceGuard is a stack-scoped RAII object; moving the identical SetRequestTraceUUID() call into the shared helper preserves the same trace scope and lifetime, so tracing behaviour is unchanged.
ledger.why: Confirms the extraction is behaviour-preserving for observability, not only for correctness.
ledger.what_i_did: Verified TraceGuard/SetRequestTraceUUID in common/log/trace.h (guard cleared on destruction) and that the helper's return point matches the wrappers' original return point, keeping the guard alive across the CreateObjectClient call.

blast_radius.why: Affects only tracing/observability, not correctness or the returned handle.

reversibility.why: The guard placement is a one-line change, trivially adjustable.

ledger.revisit_if: TraceGuard scope/lifetime semantics change in common/log/trace.h.

ledger.what: Place the shared helper in util.{h,cpp} beside CreateObjectClient rather than in either wrapper header.
ledger.why: Both wrappers already include datasystem/c_api/util.h, so the helper is reachable with no new include edges and ownership stays in the c_api util layer.
ledger.what_i_did: Specified the declaration in util.h and the definition in util.cpp; noted that util.cpp must add #include datasystem/common/log/trace.h since it is not currently included.

blast_radius.why: Reuses existing include edges; no new dependencies introduced.

reversibility.why: The helper could be relocated to another translation unit with a mechanical move.

ledger.revisit_if: The c_api util layer is reorganized.
